explanation: Various AV devices such a projector or tape recorder
video cameras here too. Some overlap or confusion with COMMUNICATIONS-CAMERA.

discriminators: Projectors, tape decks, VCRs, camcorders — these are all TRANSPORT MECHANISMS. The shared physical core is a small motor turning a reel or a sprocket, so the honest shared morphology is a PERIODIC MECHANICAL CYCLE (a film projector's ~24 Hz sprocket flutter, a tape deck's capstan whir, a VCR's head-drum whine) plus button clacks. Moderate onset_periodicity riding a motor bed.
A projector's 24 Hz shutter is the most measurable single fact in this subcategory: a strong amplitude modulation at exactly the frame rate.

But nothing in DSP distinguishes a projector from a tape recorder from a VCR — that is DEVICE IDENTITY, and it is semantic. UCS itself concedes the mess: 'Some overlap or confusion with COMMUNICATIONS-CAMERA.'
Against COMCam: UCS routes by device CLASS, not by sound — MOVIE and VIDEO cameras come HERE, STILL cameras go to COMCam. That rule is untestable from a waveform.
Against COMPhono: both are rotating media transports with motor rumble. Text ('projector', 'tape', 'VCR' versus 'turntable', 'vinyl') is the only reliable discriminator.

explanation: old flash-bulbs. Movie and video cameras to COMMUNICATIONS-AV.

discriminators: A SHUTTER IS A COMPOUND MECHANICAL IMPULSE — and that compound structure is the one genuinely measurable thing here. An SLR fires mirror-up, shutter-open, shutter-close, mirror-down inside roughly 100-250 ms, so DSP sees 2-4 TIGHTLY SPACED sharp transients, not one. transient_count of 2-4 inside a sub-300 ms window is a real, checkable signature and it is why this earns signal_narrowable rather than semantic_only.

Motor winding (auto-advance) appends a short buzzy rhythmic burst immediately after the clack — a brief periodic impulse train. Old flash-bulbs are a single burst with a bright tail and a glass tinkle.
Against COMAv: UCS routes by device, not by sound — 'Movie and video cameras to COMMUNICATIONS-AV.' Untestable from signal; text must decide.
Against MECHClik, MECHRtch and UIClick: physically the same family of small mechanical clicks. DSP can only narrow to 'small compound mechanical impulse'; the words 'camera' or 'shutter' commit it.
A PHONE camera shutter is a synthetic SAMPLE of this and will measure as UIClick — single transient, band-limited, no mechanism.

Cellphones emit SAMPLES, not physics. Ringtones, clicks and tones are synthetic and were designed to be salient, so their acoustic profile is 'a short bright tonal event, or a cyclic tonal pattern' — which is exactly what UIBeep, UIAlert, BEEP and ALRMElec are too. There is no cellphone-shaped region of feature space, and that is the honest finding.

The one weakly real cue is BAND-LIMITING at the SPEAKER: a phone's tiny transducer rolls off hard below ~300 Hz, so low_band_energy is unusually low even for a synthetic tone. Recorded CALL audio is band-limited at the top as well (~3.4 kHz), like all telephony.
Ring CADENCES are periodic (a repeating on/off pattern), which lifts onset_periodicity above a one-shot UI beep.
Vibration/haptic buzz is the exception and looks nothing like the rest of the subcategory: a low-frequency motor rattle, high low_band_energy — closer to MOTRElec.
Against COMTelph: the ONLY rule is UCS's own device rule — 'technically any telephone that isn't a mobile or cell phone' goes to TELEPHONE. Pure text. No DSP will ever adjudicate it.

explanation: Feedback and mic bumps and handling. Megaphones would go here too.

discriminators: A HETEROGENEOUS BAG — which is exactly why it must be semantic_only, and why every prior here is deliberately broad. It unions two acoustically OPPOSITE things.

(1) FEEDBACK — a Larsen howl — is one of the more separable events in all of COMMUNICATIONS: a loud, SUSTAINED, near-pure tone with a RISING envelope (loop gain builds exponentially), harmonicity > 0.85, spectral_flatness near 0, often with a slow pitch drift as one room mode hands over to another. If a file matches THAT, the call is safe.
(2) HANDLING and BUMPS — the exact opposite: low-frequency thumps and rumble dominated by low_band_energy, high crest, short. Mic handling is an IMPACT on a diaphragm; it is nearly all sub-200 Hz.

Because the subcategory unions a sustained pure tone with a low thud, NO single gate or tight prior is defensible: any gate that fits the howl would kill the bump. Split on text: 'feedback'/'Larsen' versus 'handling'/'bump'/'tap'.
Megaphones live here too: heavily band-limited, horn-loaded, distorted speech.

explanation: Communication sounds not fitting another category in this list.

discriminators: The COMMUNICATIONS catch-all. Un-gated so it remains reachable as the category's abstention target when every sibling's gates fire.

Claim it only when the file is a communications device by text but is NONE of: STATIC (stationary broadband noise), TELEMETRY (periodic coded tone or impulse train), CAMERA (compound mechanical impulse), TYPEWRITER (ringing impulse train), or one of the purely semantic device classes.

explanation: motors and mechanics. STATIC has its own subcategory here.

discriminators: A phonograph has ONE quietly beautiful measurable property, and it is worth stating precisely: its surface noise is PERIODIC AT THE ROTATION RATE. A click, pop or warp recurs ONCE PER REVOLUTION — 33 1/3 rpm = 0.56 Hz (1.8 s period), 45 rpm = 0.75 Hz (1.33 s), 78 rpm = 1.3 Hz (0.77 s). A crackle bed whose onset envelope autocorrelates with a peak at ~0.8-1.8 s IS a record, and nothing else in this ontology is. That is why onset_rate_per_second and onset_periodicity carry real weight here despite the semantic_only tier.
Underneath: a low motor rumble (turntable wow and flutter) plus discrete mechanical events — stylus drop, tonearm lift and return, the changer mechanism.

Against COMStatic: UCS explicitly routes surface noise away — 'STATIC has its own subcategory here.' A featureless, STATIONARY hiss with NO rotational periodicity is COMStatic. A crackle bed WITH rotational periodicity stays here. The autocorrelation peak is the deciding test.
Against COMAv: both are media transports with motors. Text ('turntable', 'vinyl', 'gramophone', 'Victrola' versus 'tape', 'projector') decides.

explanation: radio and shortwave sounds. STATIC has its own subcategory here. A radio broadcast would go here too. Overlap with VOICES-FUTZED.

discriminators: The real, measurable gift here is BAND-LIMITING. Broadcast radio through a receiver is bandwidth-restricted at BOTH ends — AM brutally so (roughly 100 Hz to 5 kHz, often less), FM less so. A band_limit_high_hz well below the file's Nyquist, together with a hollowed-out low band, is GENUINE, DETECTABLE evidence of a radio chain. UCS notices the consequence itself: 'Overlap with VOICES-FUTZED.'

TUNING is the other measurable behaviour, and it is distinctive: sweeping the dial produces an ALTERNATION between broadband static and briefly-locked band-limited program material. So spectral_flatness SWINGS wildly over time (between ~0.9 and ~0.4), spectral_flux is high and stationarity is low. A file whose flatness oscillates like that is being tuned, and nothing else does that.
Squelch, heterodyne whistles and carrier beats add narrow drifting tones over the static — another radio-specific tell.
Against COMStatic: UCS routes pure noise away — 'STATIC has its own subcategory here.' Inter-station hiss with NO program content is COMStatic; radio keeps the station.
Against COMTran: a broadcast radio is CONTINUOUS and one-way; a transceiver is BURSTY and half-duplex, with PTT clicks and squelch tails.
If the content is a person talking through the futz, UCS wants the VOICE in VOXFutz.

PURE BROADBAND NOISE — the single cleanest DSP target in the whole COMMUNICATIONS category, and the only reason any subcategory here is signal_separable. UCS gave STATIC its own home precisely because it is a SIGNAL TYPE, not a device: 'static from ANY electronic source — radio, CB, television, optical noise, phonograph noise.' The ontology itself is telling you to classify it by DSP.

Signature: spectral_flatness approaching 1.0 (0.8+), harmonicity approaching 0.0, spectral_flux near zero, stationarity near 1.0, sustain_ratio near 1.0. A featureless, unchanging, unpitched wall. Crest factor sits near the Gaussian value (~3-5) because that is literally what the signal is.
spectral_slope_db_per_octave sorts the flavours: white ~0, pink ~-3, brown ~-6. All are still STATIC.

Against UIGlitch — the one real trap, and it shows why flatness alone is insufficient. A glitch is EQUALLY FLAT but NOT STATIONARY: it churns (flux ~0.55, stationarity ~0.3). Static is flat AND STILL (flux ~0.08, stationarity ~0.9). Use flux and stationarity, never flatness, to separate them.

Against AIRHiss: acoustically near-identical — both are stationary broadband hiss. This is a genuine IRREDUCIBLE confusion, and the honest response is to say so: only text ('radio', 'TV', 'interference', 'noise' versus 'air', 'hiss', 'nozzle', 'valve') resolves it.
Against ELECBuzz: the noise and tonal poles. A hum is a harmonic comb; static has no comb at all.

explanation: teletype. UI is it's own category

discriminators: MACHINE-TO-MACHINE CODE — and codes are PERIODIC. Everything in this subcategory is a tone or an impulse switched ON A CLOCK, so the highest-value feature by a wide margin is onset_periodicity: high, with a clean autocorrelation peak on the onset envelope.

TELEGRAPH / MORSE is the clearest case and the most beautifully DSP-tractable thing in COMMUNICATIONS after STATIC: a RHYTHMIC IMPULSE TRAIN — a single carrier tone (or a sounder's mechanical clack) gated on and off in dots and dashes. High harmonicity, very high onset_periodicity, onset_rate ~3-15/s, and — crucially — TIGHTLY QUANTIZED inter-onset intervals, because a dash is EXACTLY three dots and the inter-element gap is exactly one dot. The distribution of event durations collapses onto a 1:3 ratio. No natural sound is quantized like that; it is a machine writing in time.

MODEM / FAX handshake is the noisy cousin: a warbling FSK/PSK carrier, still band-limited to the telephone channel (300 Hz - 3.4 kHz, hence the band_limit_high_hz prior), with HIGH spectral_flux as it hops between mark and space tones — then climbing toward noise-like flatness once the link trains up to full-rate data.
TELETYPE adds a mechanical printer: an impulse train that is simultaneously tone-coded and physically clacking.

Against UIData: the same idea in a different world. UI 'gak' is designed screen chatter and is FULL-BANDWIDTH; telemetry is a real device on a real line and is band-limited at 3.4 kHz. That band-limit is the tell.
Against COMType: a teletype straddles both. If it PRINTS, prefer COMType; if it CODES, prefer here.

explanation: Old school rotary dial and digital telephones. Technically any telephone that isn't a mobile or cell phone.

discriminators: THE TELEPHONE BAND IS REAL AND MEASURABLE: 300 Hz to 3.4 kHz. Anything that has passed through a telephone chain is HARD band-limited at BOTH ends — band_limit_high_hz ~3.4 kHz with a gutted low band. This is the most detectable single property of the subcategory, and it is shared with VOXFutz and COMTelm.

DTMF is a lovely, decisive edge case. Touch-tones are DUAL tones (e.g. '1' = 697 + 1209 Hz) whose two frequencies were deliberately chosen to be NON-HARMONICALLY related, so that no voice or line harmonic could counterfeit them. Consequence for DSP: DTMF measures as HIGH harmonicity AND HIGH inharmonicity SIMULTANEOUSLY — a combination almost nothing else in the ontology produces. If you see that pair, you are looking at a keypad.
Ring and dial tones are cyclic and periodic (US ring = 2 s on / 4 s off; US dial tone = 350 + 440 Hz continuous — itself a dual tone), which lifts onset_periodicity.

ROTARY mechanics are the other half, and they are countable: the dial's return drives a rhythmic mechanical impulse train whose PULSE COUNT EQUALS THE DIGIT DIALLED — 1 pulse for '1', 10 for '0', at exactly 10 pulses per second. A 10 Hz impulse burst of countable length is a rotary phone and genuinely nothing else.
Against COMCell: UCS's rule is pure device identity — 'technically any telephone that isn't a mobile or cell phone.' No DSP will ever adjudicate this, and the signature should never try.

explanation: TV Remote controls. STATIC has its own subcategory here. A television broadcast would go here too. Overlap with VOICES-FUTZED.

discriminators: A TELEVISION IS A LOUDSPEAKER PLAYING SOMETHING ELSE — which makes it semantic_only in the strongest sense. Its content is arbitrary (drama, news, sport, adverts, a laugh track) and its only acoustic fingerprint is the small-speaker colouration of the set plus the room it plays into. Band-limiting and a thinned low end are weak evidence at best.

TWO exceptions carry real signal content. (1) The CRT FLYBACK WHINE: a piercing, extremely stable near-pure tone at the horizontal scan rate — 15.625 kHz (PAL/625-line) or 15.734 kHz (NTSC/525-line). A stable tone at ~15.7 kHz inside an otherwise ordinary room recording is an UNMISTAKABLE, essentially decisive CRT signature, and one of the few genuinely diagnostic frequencies in this whole ontology. (2) OFF-AIR STATIC — which UCS explicitly routes elsewhere: 'STATIC has its own subcategory here.'
Channel changes and remote clicks are ordinary small synthetic/mechanical impulses. The on/off thunk and degauss of a CRT is a low-frequency burst with a brief magnetic buzz.
Speech through the set overlaps VOXFutz, as UCS notes. Put the VOICE in VOXFutz and keep the DEVICE here.

explanation: all two-way transceivers and radios go here. Put voices into VOICES-FUTZED.

discriminators: Walkie-talkies, CBs, ham rigs. Heavily BAND-LIMITED — comms audio is deliberately squeezed into ~300 Hz to 3 kHz for intelligibility, then companded and clipped on top — so band_limit_high_hz plus a near-empty low band is real, detectable evidence.

The genuinely distinctive EVENT is the SQUELCH TAIL: the burst of white noise that fires the instant the carrier drops at the end of a transmission. Acoustically it is a sharp switch from band-limited content to BROADBAND NOISE and then to SILENCE — spectral_flatness JUMPING from ~0.3 to ~0.9 within a few tens of milliseconds, then a hard gate to nothing. Combined with the PTT click at each end, a transceiver transmission has a very recognisable FRAME: [PTT click] - [band-limited content] - [squelch noise burst] - [silence]. That frame is detectable in principle.
What is NOT detectable is the DEVICE — walkie versus CB versus ham rig are the same electronics. Hence semantic_only despite the distinctive frame.
UCS routes the content away explicitly: 'Put voices into VOICES-FUTZED.' A transceiver file that is mostly speech should be VOXFutz; keep the radio's own mechanics, squelch and clicks here.
Against COMRadio: broadcast is CONTINUOUS and one-way; a transceiver is BURSTY and half-duplex. The squelch/PTT frame is the discriminator.

A METAL TYPE-BAR STRIKING A RUBBER PLATEN, at 2-6 strikes per second. The signature is an impulse train of very high-crest transients that RING — the frame, the bars and the carriage are all steel, and steel resonates. Long-ish decay_time_seconds_60db (~100-200 ms) and high inharmonicity (a metallic, non-integer ring) are the material evidence.

Against CMPTKey — the boundary UCS explicitly draws ('not modern computer keyboards'), and one of the few boundaries in COMPUTERS/COMMUNICATIONS that DSP can genuinely adjudicate. It is a MATERIAL-IMPEDANCE test, exactly what decay_time_seconds_60db exists for: a typewriter RINGS (metal, decay 100-200 ms, high inharmonicity, loud, very high crest); a plastic computer keyboard THUDS (decay < 50 ms, no ring). Trust this one.

THREE extra events seal a typewriter and have NO computer analogue: the MARGIN BELL (a clear struck-metal tone with a long harmonic ring, exactly once per line), the CARRIAGE RETURN (a fast mechanical sweep terminated by an end-stop clunk), and the platen RATCHET on line advance. A bell tone embedded in an impulse train is decisive.
Against MACHAntq: a treadle or early electric typewriter is antique machinery too — but UCS gives typewriters their own home, so prefer this on text.
Teletypes straddle COMTelm and COMType. If it PRINTS, it is here.
